ops.text: The PostToolUse:Bash "Commit landed — add a cc-task trailer / capture durable doc" hook misfired on a throwaway `git commit` inside a mktemp scratch repo under /tmp (a ccstatusline verification sandbox, not project history). The hook apparently keys on any `git commit` in the Bash command text rather than checking the commit landed in the project repo (cwd or repo root match), so it demanded a cc-task trailer for a commit that will be rm-rf'd and asserted the commit "encodes a durable doc" it had no relation to. Suggest gating that hook on the commit's repo being the session's project repo.
